titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Karen Halverson Photographs of the American West
Halverson (Karen) Photographs of the American West
titlestmt.author: by Matthew Daniel Mason

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 9618761.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday December 29, 2017 at 22:02

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: This collection includes portfolios of photographs and panoramic photographs created by Halverson that document the American West, 1987-2006.

persname.identifier: Photographs of the Garment District in New York City

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Each portfolio consists of 60 inkjet prints, 61.0 x 50.8 cm., and 4 inkjet prints, 40.6 x 121.9 cm.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition note
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Karen Halverson on the Frederick W. and Carrie S. Beinecke Fund for Western Americana, 2010-2012.

accessrestrict.head: Information about Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

userestrict.head: Ownership Copyright
userestrict.p: The Karen Halverson Photographs of the American West is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Cite As
prefercite.p: Karen Halverson Photographs of the American West. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Notes
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

bioghist.head: Karen Halverson (born 1941)
bioghist.p: Karen Halverson is a documentary landscape photographer. Her book, Downstream: Encounters with the Colorado River , was published by the University of California Press in 2008.

scopecontent.head: Description of the Papers
scopecontent.p: This collection includes portfolios of photographs and panoramic photographs created by Halverson that document the American West, 1987-2006.

Photographs of the Colorado River, 1994-1995, chronicle Halverson's exploration of the river from its tributaries, the Gunnison River and Green River, through its main length, including sites along the river in Wyoming, Colorado, Utah, Arizona, Nevada, California, and Sonora, Mexico. Many of the images were published in her work, Downstream: Encounters with the Colorado River (Berkeley: University of California Press, 2008). Images of canyons include the Canyon of Lodore, Goosenecks, Cataract Canyon, the Grand Canyon, and Black Canyon. Images of rock formations include the Sentinels in Canyonlands National Park. Images of dams include Fontenelle Dam, Glen Canyon Dam, Hoover Dam, Davis Dam, Parker Dam, Palo Verde Diversion Dam, Imperial Diversion Dam, and Morelos Dam. Images of reservoirs include Fontenelle Reservoir, Flaming Gorge Reservoir, Lake Powell, Lake Mead, Lake Mohave, and Lake Havasu, as well as a view of the natural lake, Grand Lake. Images of bridges across the Colorado River include Dewey Bridge, Navajo Bridge, and Silver Bridge.

Photographs of Basin & Range, 1987-2004, consists of images created by Halverson that explore a semiarid region of the western United States, also known as the Great Basin. This region extends from the Sierra Nevada on the west to the Wasatch Range on the east and from the Columbia Plateau in the north to the Mojave Desert in the south. Images from this region include sites in Arizona, California, Montana, Nevada, Utah, and Wyoming, as well as two views of Shiprock, New Mexico. Photographs of Arizona include Benson, Lake Powell, and Quartzite. Photographs of California include Alabama Hills, Desert Hot Springs, Independence, Keeler, Olancha, and Saline Valley, as well as several sites in Death Valley including Badwater, Death Valley Junction, Furnace Creek, and Teakettle Junction. Photographs of Montana include Dupuyer, Lennep, and an area near Heart Butte. Photographs of Nevada include the Elko, North Las Vegas, Pyramid Lake, Rhyolite, Tonopah, and Winnemucca. Photographs of Utah include Bonneville Salt Flats, Cathedral Valley in Capitol Reef National Park, Crescent Junction, Factory Butte, Hite, Great Salt Lake, Great Salt Lake Desert, Monument Valley, Price, and Valley of the Gods. Photographs of Wyoming include Cora, Dubois, Greybull, Pinedale, Reliance, Rock Springs, Thermopolis, Wind River, and Yellowstone National Park.

Mulholland: Nature and Culture in Los Angeles, 1991-2006, depicts views along Mulholland Highway and Mulholland Drive, a scenic road in Los Angeles County, California, that runs from California Highway 1 (Pacific Coast Highway) near Malibu along the crest of the Santa Monica Mountains to Hollywood. The route provides access, directly or en-route, to many of the regional parks in the Santa Monica Mountains National Recreation Area. Locations depicted in the photographs are arranged sequentially from west to east. Identified communities include Beverly Glen, Calabasas Highlands, and Universal City, as well as a view above the Hollywood Bowl. Images of canyons or their vicinity include Laurel Canyon, Fryman Canyon, and Runyon Canyon. Images of reservoirs include Encino Reservoir, Stone Canyon Reservoir, and Hollywood Reservoir. Many of the photographs show the scenic road near or at intersections with other roads.
The majority of photographs in the collection are color inkjet prints with dimensions of 61.0 x 50.8 centimeters, and the panoramic prints have dimensions 40.6 x 121.9 centimeters.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. The Colorado River, 1994-1995. II. Basin & Range, 1987-2004. III. Mulholland: Nature and Culture in Los Angeles, 1991-2006.

geogname.part: Santa Monica Mountains National Recreation Area (Calif.) -- Pictorial works.

persname.identifier: Photographs of the Garment District in New York City

arrangement.head: Arrangement note
arrangement.p: Arranged sequentially by numbers applied by Halverson.

did.unittitle: 1. Lodore Canyon, Dinosaur National Monument, Colorado
2. Split Mountain, Dinosaur National Monument, Utah
3. Browns Park National Wildlife Refuge, Colorado
8. Steamboat Rock, Dinosaur National Monument, Colorado
9. Gunnison Butte, near Green River, Utah
12. The Gooseneck, near Canyonlands National Park, Utah
13. The Gooseneck, near Canyonlands National Park, Utah
14. The Sentinels, Canyonlands National Park, Utah
15. Cataract Canyon, Canyonlands National Park, Utah
18. Under Navajo Bridge, Marble Canyon, Arizona
19. Silver Bridge, near Phantom Ranch, Grand Canyon, Arizona
26. Sheep Creek Bay Overlook, Flaming Gorge Reservoir, Utah
28. Slick Rock Canyon, Lake Powell, Utah
35. Glen Canyon Dam, near Page, Arizona
36. Glen Canyon Dam and Lake Powell, near Page, Arizona
44. Black Meadow Landing, Lake Havasu, California
46. Palo Verde Dam, near Palo Verde, California
51. Angus Wilson Bridge, from the California side of the Colorado River
52. Water Wheel Resort, near Blythe, California
57. Below Imperial Dam on the California side of the Colorado River
58. Below Imperial Dam on the California side of the Colorado River
59. Pipeline at Gene Pumping Plant just south of Parker Dam on the California side of the Colorado River

arrangement.head: Arrangement note
arrangement.p: Arranged sequentially by numbers applied by Halverson followed by panoramic photographs arranged alphabetically.

did.unittitle: 50. Lake Powell, Navajo Mountain, Navajo Power Plant, Arizona/Utah
51. Alabama Hills, near Lone Pine, California
Mulholland: Nature and Culture in Los Angeles

arrangement.head: Arrangement note
arrangement.p: Arranged sequentially by numbers applied by Halverson followed by panoramic photographs also arranged sequentially.
